what_it_does: This file defines an MCP tool, cc_channel, that exposes live cross-session messaging into running Claude Code sessions and a named channel registry. It delegates all operations to runtime/cc_channels.py: listing live sessions, sending 1:1 messages, broadcasting to many sessions under a shared thread, reading a mail log, and creating/listing/archiving named channels plus adding/removing members.
observations: The tool is registered via a register(mcp, suite) function using @mcp.tool()
All runtime logic is imported lazily inside the tool function (from runtime import cc_channels as cc)
The op parameter is typed as a Literal covering nine operations
Broadcast splits to by comma, generates a group thread id from time if none provided, and catches ChannelError per target
Mail supports an optional thread filter and a limit
Named channel ops (create_channel, list_channels, add_member, remove_member, archive_channel) use separate parameters (name, channel, handle, purpose, coordinator, include_archived)
ChannelError is caught and returned as an ok=False dict; unknown ops raise ValueError
standouts: Live injection into hand-launched Claude Code sessions through MCP
Broadcast sends one message to many sessions under a single shared thread
Named channel registry is managed separately from live transport (members are reached at push time elsewhere)
Replies are pushed back rather than polled
conventions: File-drop tool pattern with an explicit register function
Runtime module imported inside the tool body rather than at top level
Extensive docstring doubles as user-facing operation spec
Literal type hint enumerates allowed operations
concerns: Broadcast only catches cc.ChannelError per target; other exceptions would abort the whole broadcast
Thread id for broadcast uses int(time.time()), which is not guaranteed unique under rapid calls
No validation that op matches the Literal at runtime beyond the type hint
The runtime module is imported inside the function, so missing dependencies surface only at call time
No escaping/validation of comma-separated target strings beyond strip()
notes: A single MCP tool surface that lets the fabric discover, message, and group live Claude Code sessions, backed by runtime/cc_channels.py.
questions: What is the exact API contract of runtime/cc_channels.py?
How are live sessions discovered and how is handle/cwd resolution implemented?
How are replies pushed back to the caller without polling?
What is the mail log storage format and persistence model?
What does session_post wake/consult mean for closed sessions?
How does the named channel registry interact with the transport layer at push time?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it exposes live session messaging and named channel registry operations through an MCP tool.
apparent_themes: MCP tool surfacing of runtime capabilities
Named group registry with archival semantics
Thread continuity for broadcast and reply tracking
intention_signals: Enable the fabric to reach into running Claude Code sessions, coordinate multi-session conversations, and maintain persistent named channels for repeated group communication.
novelty: Direct MCP-mediated injection into live Claude Code conversations with push-back replies, plus a lightweight named channel registry that survives independently of transport state.
contribution: Provides an MCP tool endpoint that exposes live session discovery, 1:1 and broadcast messaging, mail log reading, and named channel lifecycle management, delegating execution to runtime/cc_channels.py.
summary_for_embedding: An MCP tool file that exposes live Claude Code session messaging (list, send, broadcast, mail) and a named channel registry (create, list, add/remove member, archive) backed by runtime/cc_channels.py.
intention_for_embedding: To give the fabric an MCP tool surface for injecting messages into running Claude Code sessions and managing persistent broadcast groups across them.

symbols.does: Registers the cc_channel MCP tool with the provided mcp instance.
symbols.description: Decorated with @mcp.tool(), this function defines the tool signature and operation dispatch, binding live-session messaging and named channel management to the MCP surface.

symbols.does: Dispatches the requested op to runtime/cc_channels.py functions for live session messaging and channel registry operations.
symbols.description: The actual MCP tool callable; validates required arguments per operation, invokes cc_channels.live_sessions/send/mail/create_channel/etc., catches ChannelError, and returns structured dictionary results.
